claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

**Do you make financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or somewhere to live)? And if so, how much do you contribute per week or month?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards his upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and bits and bobs for him. I give about £10 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to check one more thing:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, the person he lives with)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, the person he lives with already claims Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16, so age-qualified)
- You live in the UK
- Alex doesn't live with you
- You contribute £10 per week towards his upkeep
- Someone else is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex

The key issue here is that **only one person can get Child Benefit for a child**. Since someone else is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot also claim it for him.

Additionally, even if the other person stopped claiming, your contribution of £10 per week would need to be assessed. To qualify for Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, you must contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (which is currently over £20 per week for one child). Your contribution of £10 per week appears to fall short of this threshold.

Unfortunately, based on your circumstances, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if Alex comes to live with you, or if the other person stops claiming and you're able to increase your contributions - you may become eligible. In that case, you could contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and the claimant lives in the UK, which meets the basic criteria. However, Alex does not live with the claimant. While the claimant contributes £10 per week towards Alex's upkeep, someone else is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under the rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Additionally, to continue receiving Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, contributions must equal or exceed the Child Benefit payment amount. The claimant's £10 weekly contribution appears insufficient compared to the current Child Benefit rate. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who does not live with them. Another person is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since only one person can claim Child Benefit per child, and the claimant's financial contributions (£10/week) appear below the threshold required when a child lives elsewhere, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

Additionally, to continue receiving Child Benefit when a child lives elsewhere, contributions must equal or exceed the Child Benefit payment amount. The claimant's £10 weekly contribution appears insufficient compared to the current Child Benefit rate. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
